steps.title: Identify the core claim
steps.content: The conclusion states that irrigation response is “highly sensitive to the baseline concentration” — meaning that when precipitation becomes concentrated in the future, the irrigation increase depends critically on what the baseline precipitation concentration currently is.

steps.title: Find data showing baseline dependence
steps.content: B directly shows this: when baseline is somewhat concentrated, irrigation increases only slightly; when baseline is evenly distributed, irrigation increases substantially (9.0% for surface water, 7.9% for groundwater). The other choices either describe just one baseline scenario, confuse different water types, or contradict the conclusion by claiming irrigation declines. That matches choice B — B is correct.
